title: Narrative Arc — Media Blitz Is the Publishing Subsystem
recognition: Publishing efforts often begin with visible platforms, cadence, and output.
reframe: Visibility is not architecture; publishing is downstream of durable knowledge manufacturing.
framework: Source material, extraction, canonical artifacts, corpus, publication engine, and platform derivatives form distinct layers.
proof: The Nelson Knowledge System repository already separates corpus, governance, runtime, visual, publication, and derivative records.
application: Use Media Blitz OS to convert governed canonical knowledge into articles, posts, threads, diagrams, and campaign packages.
consequence: When publishing remains a subsystem, each public artifact can strengthen and reuse the larger knowledge architecture.
invitation: Evaluate publishing systems by the knowledge supply chain behind them, not by output volume alone.
